step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with option C because it correctly describes how reciprocal clasps work — they oppose the movement that occurs when the retentive arm locks into place, thereby maintaining denture stability under functional loads. This action prevents excessive mobility and improves overall performance of the prosthesis.

---

Agent2: I also agree with Option C – "To counteract the movement of denture which is caused during engagement of retentive arm." 

The reciprocal clasp arm functions not just passively but actively opposes any displacement initiated by the retentive arm’s seating. This dynamic opposition ensures better control over the denture's position during mastication and speech, reducing the risk of disengagement or instability.

---

Agent3: I agree with **Option C**, as well. Reciprocal clasps provide additional stabilization through their ability to resist unwanted motion created when the retentive arm engages. They do this by creating an opposing force against the movement induced by the retentive arm itself—thus helping maintain proper positioning throughout functional activities such as eating and talking.

---

Final consensus among agents: All agents agreed on **C**
